title: Medical system for detecting an atrial tachyarrhythmia through classification based on heart rate variability
abstract: A medical device (60) for detecting an atrial tachyarrhythmia comprises a processor configured to determine RR intervals between successive R-waves of a cardiac electrical signal and to determine classification factors from the R-waves identified over a predetermined time period by determining at least a first classification factor correlated to variability of the RR intervals and a second classification factor indicating a presence of a ventricular tachyarrhythmia. The processor is configured to classify the cardiac electrical signal of the predetermined time period as unclassified, atrial tachyarrhythmia or non-atrial tachyarrhythmia based on a comparison of the determined classification factors to classification criteria.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A medical device system for detecting an atrial tachyarrhythmia, comprising: sensing circuitr - configured to sense a first cardiac electrical signal from a first sensing electrode vector; a processor; and memory accessible by the processor, the processor configured to: identify R-waves in the first cardiac electrical signal attendant ventricular depolarizations; determine RR intervals between successive R-waves in the first cardiac electrical signal; determine classification factors from the R-waves identified over a first predetermined time period by determining at least a first classification factor correlated to variability of the RR intervals and a second classification factor indicating a presence of a ventricular tachyarrhythmia; classify the first predetermined time period as one of unclassified, atrial tachyarrhythmia and non-atrial tachyarrhythmia by comparing the determined classification factors to classification criteria, wherein the first predetermined time period is classified as unclassified in response to the second classification factor indicating the presence of a ventricular tachyarrhythmia; detect an atrial tachyarrhythmia episode in response to the first time period being classified as unclassified and at least one preceding time period and at least one subsequent time period being classified as atrial tachyarrhythmia; and in response to detecting the atrial tachyarrhythmia episode, storing episode data determined from at least the first cardiac electrical signal in the memory for transmission to an external device. 2. The medical device system of claim 1 1, wherein the processor is configured to: determine a third classification factor by detecting ventricular pacing pulses during the first predetermined time period; determine a count of the detected ventricular pacing pulses during the first predetermined time period; and 

 classify the first predetermined time period as unclassified in response to the count exceeding a threshold. 3. The medical device system of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the processor is configured to determine the second classification factor by detecting a ventricular tachyarrhythmia based on an analysis of the identified R-waves. 4. The medical device system of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the processor is configured to detect the atrial tachyarrhythmia by: classifying a plurality of successive predetermined time periods comprising the first time period, the at least one preceding time period and the at least one subsequent time period; increasing an atrial tachyarrhythmia counter in response to each of the plurality ' of successive predetermined time periods that is classified as atrial tachyarrhythmia; and detecting the atrial tachyarrhythmia in response to the atrial tachyarrhythmia counter reaching an atrial tachyarrhythmia detection threshold. 5. The medical device system of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the processor is further configured to: increase an unclassified counter in response to each of the plu rality of successiv e predetermined time periods that is classified as unclassified; compare the unclassified counter to an unclassified threshold; and reset both of the atrial tachyarrhythmia counter and the unclassifi ed counter to zero in response to the unclassified counter reaching the unclassified threshold prior to the atrial tachyarrhythmia counter reaching the atrial tachyarrhythmia detection tlireshold. 6. The medical device system of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the processor is further configured to: classify subsequent time periods after detecting the atrial tachyarrhythmia; increase an unclassified counter in response to each one of the subsequent time periods that is classified as unclassified; 

 reset the unclassified counter to zero in response to classifying one of the subsequent time periods as atrial fibrillation; compare the unclassified counter to an unclassified threshold upon classifying each of the subsequent time periods; and detect termination of the atrial tachyarrhythmia episode in response to the unclassified counter reaching the unclassified threshold, 7. The medical device system of claim 6, wherein the processor is further configured to determine an episode duration of the detected atrial tachyarrhythmia episode in response to detecting termination, wherein determining the episode duration comprises excluding from the episode duration the subsequent time periods classified as unclassified after the last one of the subsequent time periods classified as atrial tachyarrhythmia. 8. The medical device system of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the processor is further configured to: detect termination of the atrial tachyarrhythmia episode; wherein the processor is configured to store the episode data by determining a time duration of the detected atrial tachyarrhythmia episode, wherein the time duration includes the first time period. 9. The medical device system of any one of claims 1-8, wherein: the sensing circuitry is further configured to sense a second cardiac electrical signal from a second sensing electrode vector different than the first sensing electrode vector; responsive to detecting the atrial tachyarrhythmia, the processor is further configured to: store a first episode of the first cardiac electrical signal in the memory at a first gain; and store a second episode of the second cardiac electrical signal in the memory at a second gam that is higher than the first gam. 10. The medical device system of claim 9, further comprising: 

 an external device comprising an external telemetry circuit and a display; an implantable telemetry circuit coupled to the processor and configured to: receive an interrogation command from the external device; and transmit the first cardiac electrical signal having the first gain and the second cardiac signal having the second gain to the external device; the external device configured to display the first cardiac electrical signal at the first gain and the second cardiac electrical signal at the second gain. 1 1. The medical device system, of any one of claims 1-10, further compri sing: an electrical stimulation lead carrying at least one of the electrodes of the first sensing vector; and an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) coupled to the electrical stimulation lead, the ICD comprising: therapy circuitry configured to generate an electrical stimulation therapy in response to detecting the ventricular tachyarrhythmia being one of ventricular tachycardia (VT) or ventricular fibrillation (VF); and a housing that encloses the sensing circuitry, therapy circuitry, processor, and memory.
